# Data Science Revision Journey

This repository contains my complete revision journey for Data Science, which I started from **April 3, 2026**.
The purpose of this repository is to strengthen my understanding of the core concepts required in Data Science and Machine Learning by revisiting everything from the fundamentals.
I believe strong fundamentals create strong projects and better problem-solving skills.  
For the next few months, I will be consistently revising and practicing topics that are essential for building a career in Data Science and AI/ML.

This repository will serve as my learning journal, revision notebook, and practice workspace.

## Why I Created This Repository

As I continue my journey toward becoming a Data Scientist / AI-ML professional, I realized that revision is just as important as learning new topics.

This repository is created to:

- Revise Python fundamentals
- Practice important Python libraries
- Strengthen mathematical concepts
- Improve data handling and analysis skills
- Build consistency in daily learning
- Track my progress over time

Instead of only learning new concepts, I want to make sure I deeply understand the basics and can apply them confidently in real-world projects.

---

## Topics Covered

This repository will include revision and practice materials for the following areas:

### Python Fundamentals
- Variables and Data Types
- Operators
- Conditional Statements
- Loops
- Functions
- Lambda Functions
- List Comprehension
- Iterators and Generators
- Exception Handling
- File Handling
- Object-Oriented Programming
- Modules and Packages
- Regular Expressions

---

### Python Libraries for Data Science
- NumPy
- Pandas
- Matplotlib
- Seaborn
- Scikit-learn
- Statsmodels

Topics covered include:

- Data manipulation
- Data cleaning
- Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
- Data visualization
- Statistical analysis
- Feature engineering
- Model building

---

### Mathematics for Data Science
A strong mathematical foundation is essential in Data Science, so I am also revising:

- Linear Algebra
- Probability
- Statistics
- Mean, Median, Mode
- Variance and Standard Deviation
- Correlation and Covariance
- Matrices and Vectors
- Calculus basics
- Gradient concepts
- Optimization basics

---

### Machine Learning Concepts
As part of revision, I will also revisit important ML concepts:

- Supervised Learning
- Unsupervised Learning
- Regression
- Classification
- Clustering
- Train-Test Split
- Model Evaluation Metrics
- Overfitting and Underfitting
- Cross Validation
- Feature Selection

---

## Learning Approach

My revision strategy is simple:

- Learn concept
- Practice with code
- Solve small tasks
- Apply on mini datasets
- Revise notes
- Repeat regularly

This helps me move from theory to practical understanding.

---
